member.office_address: 1519 Longworth House Office Building Washington DC 20515-2210

form_analysis.form_header_text: FY27 Appropriations Request Form (Programmatic and Language)
form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
Rep. John James. My office accepts appropriations requests from interested parties. 

For your request to be given consideration, you must complete the form below for each programmatic funding and/or bill or report language request. Multiple requests in one submission WILL NOT be accepted. Please submit each request individually. 

Our deadline for Fiscal Year 2027 appropriations requests is forthcoming. 

Please contact my office at PolicyRepJames@mail.house.gov should you have any questions.

sections.description: Please provide the contact information for the person or organization submitting this request.

fields.label: Name:
fields.help_text: Name of the person submitting the request

fields.label: Title/Role
fields.help_text: Submitter's title or role within the organization

fields.label: Organization (if applicable):
fields.help_text: Full legal name of the organization requesting the funding or language

fields.label: Email:
fields.help_text: Primary email for correspondence regarding this request

sections.description: Information regarding the specific appropriations request.

fields.label: Request Type:
fields.help_text: Indicate if this is for programmatic funding or bill/report language
fields.rationale: The request is for a specific funding amount ($9 billion) for the NASA Science Mission Directorate, which is defined as a programmatic request in the provided context.

fields.label: Appropriations Subcommittee Designation
fields.help_text: Select the subcommittee with jurisdiction over this request
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education
fields.rationale: NASA funding falls under the jurisdiction of the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies Subcommittee.

fields.label: Title of your request:
fields.help_text: Provide a concise title for the request
fields.rationale: Used the standardized 'Short Title' from the reference values as per instructions.

fields.label: Federal Agency to which the request pertains:
fields.rationale: Directly specified in the provided reference values.

fields.label: Account within the Agency to which the request pertains:
fields.help_text: The specific federal account to be funded or directed
fields.rationale: The 'Account Full Name' provided in reference values identifies the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Proposed language:
fields.help_text: The specific bill or report language requested
fields.draft_value:
* Provide $9,000,000,000 for NASA Science Mission Directorate (SMD) to support planetary science, astrophysics, Earth science, and heliophysics.
* This programmatic request seeks to restore funding levels following the disruptions of the FY2026 budget cycle.
* Ensure sufficient funding for decadal survey priorities to maintain U.S. leadership in space exploration and scientific discovery.
fields.rationale: As this is a programmatic request for the SMD topline, the language reflects the $9 billion ask and the need for decadal priority stability.

sections.description: Identify the nature of the request and provide relevant funding history.

fields.label: Request type
fields.help_text: Categorize the request as Defense or Non-Defense
fields.rationale: NASA's Science Mission Directorate is a civilian agency program and is categorized as Non-Defense discretionary spending.

fields.label: Current and Previous Budget Numbers (Non-Defense)
fields.help_text: Provide dollar figures for current and prior year funding for Non-Defense requests
fields.rationale: Provides the requested FY27 amount alongside the authoritative enacted totals from FY25 and FY26 provided in the reference data.

fields.label: Current and Previous Budget Numbers (Defense-Only)
fields.help_text: Provide dollar figures for current and prior year funding for Defense-Only requests
fields.rationale: This is a non-defense request falling under the NASA Science Mission Directorate; this section is not applicable.

fields.label: Defense and MILCON Requests (Only)
fields.help_text: Additional details specific to Defense and Military Construction requests
fields.rationale: This request is for NASA SMD (Non-Defense/CJS), so this section is not applicable.

sections.description: Provide a narrative explanation for the request and information on coordination with other offices.

fields.label: Describe the purpose and impact of your request. How will this request help your organization, Michigan and the nation?
fields.draft_value:
* Secures U.S. leadership in the space race against China, ensuring the United States sets the norms for 21st-century exploration and planetary defense.
* Supports a high-tech workforce and STEM pipeline that benefits Michigan's industrial and innovation sectors through indirect aerospace demand.
* Delivers a 3-to-1 economic return on investment, advancing critical technologies in weather forecasting, resource management, and telecommunications.
* Restores funding stability to the scientific community following proposed cuts in previous fiscal years, ensuring the U.S. remains the world leader in space science.
fields.rationale: Focuses on national leadership and economic impacts, framing NASA science as a strategic investment for the nation and Michigan's broader industrial base.

fields.label: Describe the support your request has from the community
fields.help_text: Evidence of local or national stakeholders who support this request
fields.draft_value:
* Backed by a bipartisan coalition in the House, including leads Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA).
* Supported by major scientific and professional organizations including the American Astronomical Society (AAS) and American Geophysical Union (AGU).
* Reflects the scientific community's peer-reviewed consensus established through the National Academies' Decadal Surveys.
fields.rationale: Lists the existing bipartisan support in the House and the major allied scientific organizations identified in the reference values.

fields.label: List any other Members of the U.S. House and Senate who are receiving this request.
fields.help_text: Indicate if this request is being submitted to other congressional offices
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request. Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.
